name: Finland (state and war flag)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic gold of the lion
the heraldic red of the shield

description: Swallow-tailed and tongued variant of the Finnish blue Nordic cross on white, with the national coat of arms (a crowned lion on a red shield) at the cross intersection.
history: Adopted on 29 May 1918 as the war flag and naval ensign of newly independent Finland; the swallow-tail and the coat-of-arms shield distinguish it from the civil flag.

features.role: Coat of arms of Finland (a crowned lion on a red shield)
